title: Username and Password Authentication
description: The system shall authenticate users via username and password credentials.
rationale: Secure user authentication is required to protect student data and enforce role-based access control across the platform.

title: Role-Based Access Control
description: The system shall enforce role-based access control distinguishing between student and admin roles.
rationale: Different user roles have fundamentally different capabilities; students must not be able to access or invoke admin-only functions.

title: Admin Account Provisioning
description: The system shall allow admin users to create and modify student and admin accounts.
rationale: Administrators need direct control over user access to manage onboarding and account configuration as the user base grows.

title: Admin Account Deactivation
description: The system shall allow admin users to deactivate student and admin accounts.
rationale: Offboarding and revoking access must be possible without permanently deleting records, to support audit trails and re-activation if needed.

title: Admin Course Oversight
description: The system shall allow admin users to view all courses and their statuses system-wide.
rationale: Admins need visibility into all active, archived, and completed courses for support, quality assurance, and operational monitoring.

title: AI API Credential Configuration
description: The system shall authenticate to the external AI API using credentials supplied via environment variables.
rationale: Hardcoded credentials are exposed in source control, container images, and logs; supplying secrets exclusively via environment variables eliminates these exposure vectors.

title: Single Active Course Per Student
description: The system shall restrict each student to one active course at a time.
rationale: Limiting to one active course ensures students maintain focus and allows the platform to deliver a high-quality, undivided personalized learning experience.

title: Chair Intake Questionnaire
description: The University Chair agent shall conduct a structured intake questionnaire before generating a course syllabus.
rationale: Sufficient intake questioning is required to generate a course plan genuinely personalized to the student's knowledge level, learning objectives, and time availability.

title: Dynamic Grade Distribution
description: The University Chair agent shall determine the point distribution across homework and the final project based on the course syllabus.
rationale: Different topics and learners require different grade weightings; a fixed distribution would poorly serve students whose learning needs vary significantly across disciplines.

title: Upfront Course Content Generation
description: The system shall generate all lesson content and homework assignments after the student approves the syllabus.
rationale: Generating content upfront ensures students have immediate access to their full course plan and eliminates wait times during active study sessions.

title: Content Library Reuse with Student Consent
description: The University Chair agent shall require explicit student consent before reusing or deriving content from any existing course content in the system's content library.
rationale: Student consent ensures that reused or derived material is appropriate and acceptable for the student's specific learning context before content generation proceeds.

title: Source Citation in Lesson Content
description: The system shall require all generated lesson content to include cited sources.
rationale: Citations give students verifiable references and allow instructors to audit content accuracy against current research.

title: Agent Internet Search Capability
description: The system shall enable content-generation agents to search the internet to retrieve current information.
rationale: Internet search access ensures agents can ground content in current research rather than stale training data, which is essential for rapidly evolving subject areas.

title: Agent Pipeline Transparency
description: The system shall display the real-time status of the multi-agent content generation pipeline to students.
rationale: Surfacing pipeline status builds student trust by making the content generation and verification process visible and auditable rather than a black box.

title: Agent Correction Loop Limit
description: The system shall limit the content correction loop to an admin-configurable maximum number of iterations before escalating the failure to an admin.
rationale: An unbounded correction loop would block content delivery indefinitely; a configurable cap ensures persistent failures surface for human resolution rather than silently stalling the student.

title: Homework and Project File Submission
description: The system shall accept homework and final project submissions as file uploads in LaTeX, Markdown, or AsciiDoc formats.
rationale: Structured text formats allow agents to accurately parse and grade submissions without manual transcription; restricting to AI-ingestible formats is required for automated grading to function correctly.

title: Late Penalty Enforcement
description: The system shall apply a five-percent grade deduction per calendar day to submissions received after the agreed due date.
rationale: A consistent and predictable late penalty encourages on-time submission and reflects standard academic grading practice agreed to by the student at course planning time.

title: Badge Award for Academic Milestones
description: The system shall award badges to students upon reaching defined academic milestones.
rationale: Recognizing academic milestones with badges provides positive reinforcement and creates visible progress markers that encourage continued engagement.

title: Natural Language Chat Interface
description: The system shall provide a natural language chat interface through the University Chair agent throughout the course lifecycle, including intake, syllabus approval, and active study.
rationale: A chat interface is the primary mechanism for delivering personalized learning and allows students to address comprehension gaps and adapt the course in real time.

title: Content Export to PDF and HTML
description: The system shall allow students and admin users to export any course content to PDF or standalone HTML format.
rationale: Students need portable versions of course content for offline study; admins need export capability for quality review and support purposes.

title: Student Data Privacy
description: The system shall restrict access to a student's course data, submissions, grades, and chat history to that student and admin users.
rationale: Student academic data is sensitive; strict per-student data isolation prevents unauthorized access, maintains trust in the platform, and is a prerequisite for responsible multi-user operation.

title: Authenticated Session Expiry
description: The system shall invalidate a user's session after a configurable period of inactivity.
rationale: Automatic session expiry limits the window of exposure if a session token is compromised or a user forgets to log out on a shared device; the period must be configurable so operators can balance security and usability for their deployment.

title: Student Account Data Deletion
description: The system shall permanently delete all personal data associated with a student account upon an authorized deletion request.
rationale: Users have a right to erasure of their personal data; permanent deletion on request is a baseline obligation for any platform handling personal academic records.

title: Password Reset
description: The system shall allow a user to reset their password via a verified out-of-band confirmation.
rationale: Users will inevitably forget credentials; a secure reset path prevents permanent account lockout without exposing accounts to unauthorized takeover.

title: Course Archival on Dropout
description: The system shall archive a student's course when the student withdraws from it.
rationale: Students may need to pause their studies due to external circumstances; archiving prevents data loss and preserves course state for potential re-engagement.

title: Archived Course Resumption
description: The system shall allow a student to resume a previously archived course.
rationale: Allowing resumption reduces friction for students returning after a break and protects prior learning investment by restoring their exact course state.

title: Syllabus Modification Request
description: The system shall allow students to request modifications to a generated course syllabus.
rationale: Students must have agency over their course plan to ensure content and structure meet their actual learning goals before generation begins.

title: Syllabus Approval Gate
description: The system shall finalize a course syllabus only after the student explicitly approves it.
rationale: Requiring explicit approval prevents course content generation from proceeding on a syllabus the student has not accepted, avoiding wasted generation and misaligned expectations.

title: Chair-Assigned Due Dates
description: The University Chair agent shall assign homework due dates based on the approved course syllabus.
rationale: Due dates must be derived from the course structure to ensure a consistent and realistic schedule that reflects the student's agreed study plan.

title: Student Agreement on Due Date Schedule
description: The system shall require student agreement before finalizing the homework due date schedule.
rationale: Requiring explicit agreement ensures the student commits to a realistic plan that accounts for their availability, reducing disputes and missed deadlines.

title: Section-Level Student Feedback
description: The system shall allow students to submit feedback on course content at the end of each section.
rationale: Section-level feedback gives students a structured mechanism to signal comprehension gaps or dissatisfaction before the next section begins.

title: Section Content Regeneration on Feedback
description: The system shall regenerate a course section's content when a student submits feedback requesting a change.
rationale: Personalized learning requires the ability to refine content in response to student comprehension; section-level regeneration keeps the course aligned with the student's evolving needs.

title: AI API Failure Student Notification
description: The system shall notify the student when an AI API call fails after exhausting the configured retry limit.
rationale: Silent failures during AI-driven content generation would leave students in an undefined state; explicit notification ensures users know to contact support.

title: AI API Failure Content Generation Halt
description: The system shall halt content generation when an AI API call fails after exhausting the configured retry limit.
rationale: Continuing to generate content after an unrecoverable API failure risks producing incomplete or corrupted course material; halting prevents data corruption and undefined system state.

title: Login Brute-Force Protection
description: The system shall lock a user account for a configurable period after five consecutive failed authentication attempts.
rationale: Locking accounts after repeated failures prevents automated credential-stuffing and brute-force attacks from compromising user accounts.

title: Password-Reset Rate Limiting
description: The system shall limit password-reset requests to a maximum of three requests per account per hour.
rationale: Rate-limiting password resets prevents abuse of the reset flow as an account enumeration or denial-of-service vector against legitimate users.

title: TLS Enforcement
description: The system shall transmit all client-server communication exclusively over TLS.
rationale: Unencrypted transmission exposes authentication tokens, student submissions, and personal data to interception; TLS is the baseline transport security requirement.

title: Password Storage Security
description: The system shall store user passwords using an adaptive cryptographic hash function.
rationale: Storing passwords in plaintext or with non-adaptive hashes renders the credential store trivially exploitable if the database is compromised; adaptive hashing raises the cost of offline attacks as hardware improves.

title: Admin Action Audit Logging
description: The system shall record a tamper-evident audit log entry for every create, modify, deactivate, delete, and policy-change action performed by an admin user.
rationale: Tamper-evident audit logs provide accountability for privileged actions and are required for security incident investigation and compliance review.

title: Content Generation Time Bound Notification
description: The system shall notify the student if upfront course content generation does not complete within a configurable time limit.
rationale: Silent long-running generation leaves students uncertain about system state; a configurable timeout notification allows students to take action and enables operators to tune the threshold.

title: AI API Rate-Limit Backoff
description: The system shall detect API rate-limit responses from the AI provider and apply exponential backoff before retrying.
rationale: Immediate retries on rate-limit responses worsen the throttling condition; exponential backoff respects provider quotas and increases the probability of successful retry without manual intervention.

title: Safe Termination Before Account Deletion
description: The system shall safely terminate any in-flight content generation operations for a student account before executing a deletion request.
rationale: Deleting an account while generation is in progress risks partial writes, orphaned resources, and inconsistent data state; orderly termination first ensures a clean deletion.

title: AI Processing Consent
description: The system shall obtain explicit student consent to send their submitted content and chat history to the external AI provider before processing begins.
rationale: Students must understand and agree that their data is transmitted to a third-party AI provider; informed consent is a legal and ethical obligation before such data transfer occurs.

title: Health Check Endpoint
description: The system shall expose a health check endpoint that returns the operational status of all critical subsystems.
rationale: A machine-readable health endpoint enables load balancers, orchestrators, and monitoring systems to detect and respond to subsystem failures without manual intervention.

title: CSRF Protection
description: The system shall validate CSRF tokens on all state-changing requests.
rationale: Session-based authentication is vulnerable to cross-site request forgery; CSRF token validation prevents malicious third-party sites from issuing authenticated requests on behalf of a logged-in user.

title: Per-Student AI API Cost Limit
description: The system shall enforce a configurable maximum AI API token expenditure per student per course.
rationale: Unlimited AI API calls per student create unbounded operational cost; a configurable per-course cap ensures the platform remains economically viable and prevents a single student from consuming API quota across all users.

title: Admin Grading Policy Configuration
description: The system shall allow admin users to configure grading policies including late-penalty rules and grade-weight distributions.
rationale: Grading policy must be tunable by operators to match institutional rules without code changes; late penalties and weight distributions are independent academic policy concerns that must be separately controllable.

title: Admin Agent Retry Limit Configuration
description: The system shall allow admin users to configure agent retry limits.
rationale: Agent retry limits control operational cost and latency; making them configurable allows operators to tune the system for their infrastructure without code changes.

title: Badge Final Grade Improvement Reward
description: The system shall apply final grade improvements as rewards for earned badges.
rationale: Grade improvements tied to badges provide a tangible academic incentive that meaningfully rewards students who achieve defined milestones beyond cosmetic recognition.

title: Badge Late-Penalty Waiver Reward
description: The system shall apply late-penalty waivers as rewards for earned badges.
rationale: Late-penalty waivers tied to badges give students a meaningful incentive to earn milestones early, and this reward type is operationally distinct from grade improvements and must be independently verifiable.

title: File Upload Size Validation
description: The system shall reject file submissions that exceed the configured maximum file size.
rationale: Oversized file uploads can exhaust storage and processing resources, enabling denial-of-service conditions; enforcing a size limit at the boundary protects platform stability.

title: File Upload Format Validation
description: The system shall reject file submissions that do not conform to the declared file format.
rationale: Malformed or misrepresented files can cause content-injection risks and automated grading failures; format validation at the boundary ensures only parseable submissions reach the grading pipeline.
